name: kettlebell alternating hang clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Allow the kettlebells to hang in front of your body with your arms fully extended.
In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the kettlebells up towards your shoulders.
As the kettlebells reach shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebells in the rack position, with your palms facing inward and the kettlebells resting on the outside of your forearms.
Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat the movement with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip. Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up. Allow the kettlebells to hang in front of your body with your arms fully extended. In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the kettlebells up towards your shoulders. As the kettlebells reach shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebells in the rack position, with your palms facing inward and the kettlebells resting on the outside of your forearms. Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat the movement with the opposite arm. Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
